Pesach Conversion and Shelamim Leftovers
Nazirite and Communal Nazirite Fund Leftovers

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Rabbi Yosi's Account of Rav Yehuda's Question on Shekel Death

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yosi Heard Rav Yehuda Ask Shmuel About Sanctified Shekel at Death
Distinction Between Mistake (Ta'us) and Legitimate Sanctification

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Dispute over Tenth of Ephah Leftovers - Rabbi Yochanan vs Rabbi Eliezer

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan Rules Asirit HaEifah Leftovers Thrown to Dead Sea
Mishnah Contradicts Rabbi Yochanan - Leftovers Go to Nedavah
Rabbi Yochanan's Resolution: Mishnah Discusses Minchat Choteh of Olah V'Yored

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Babylonian Derivation of Pesach Conversion to Shelamim

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yosi Reports Babylonian Derasha: 'Anything from Flocks Becomes Shelamim'
Abba bar Ba Challenges: What About Olah from Flocks?
Resolution: Min Hatzon Excludes Asham, Includes Only Pesach
Logical Arguments: Eating vs Non-Eating and Kodashim Kalim vs Kodashei Kodashim

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Shechita Conditions for Pesach Conversion - Shelamim vs Other Offerings

micro_segments.title: Babylonian Claim: Pesach Becomes Shelamim Only if Slaughtered for Shelamim
Rabbi Chanina's Alternative: Pesach Becomes Shelamim for Any Non-Pesach Intent
Rav Illa's Explanation: Pesach Becomes Shelamim through Any Korban Intent

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Pesach Slaughter After Pesach - Lishmo and She'lo Lishmo Analysis

micro_segments.title: Rule: After Pesach, Slaughter She'lo Lishmo for Pesach to Convert to Shelamim
Rabbi Avin: Lishmo and She'lo Lishmo Neutralize When Undesignated
Question: Does Dual Intent (Lishmo and She'lo Lishmo) Neutralize Each Other?
Challenge: If Neutralized, Why Doesn't Separated Intent Also Neutralize?
Abba Mari Challenges Assumption That Silence Without Intent Is Valid

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Nazirite Leftovers - Animal Korbanos vs Bread and Wine

micro_segments.title: Rav Chisda: Nazirite Leftovers Identified by Order of Purchase/Offering
Rav Zeira Disagrees: All Nazirite Leftovers Treated as Sin Offering Leftovers
Baraita Support: Mixed Funds with Chatat Money Always Deemed Chatat Leftovers
Baraita Objection: Mixture of Shelamim and Olah Funds Produces No Meila if Partial Use

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Nazirite Bread and Wine Leftovers - Cannot Reuse as Same Offering

micro_segments.title: Rav Chisda: Nazirite Bread Leftovers Must Be Left to Rot
Explanation: Bread Offering Unique to Each Nazirite, Cannot Transfer or Repeat
Rav Yosef's Thought: Wine Leftovers Should Also Rot Like Bread
Rav Yossi bar Rabbi Bun: Wine Leftovers Are Kodashei Kodashim, Become Nedavah

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Three Authorities Agree - Kodashei Kodashim Leftovers Become Nedavah

micro_segments.title: Shmuel on Shekel Leftovers, Rabbi Eliezer on Kohen Gadol Mincha, Rabbi Yosi bar Bun Interpretation

macro_segments.title: Mishnah: Leftovers for Captive Ransom, Poor, and Dead

micro_segments.title: Captive Ransom: Communal Leftovers Saved; Individual Captive's Leftovers Go to Him
Poor: Communal Funds Saved; Individual's Excess Goes to That Poor Person
Dead: Communal Burial Funds Saved; Individual's Excess Disputed by Tannaim

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Dead Person's Burial Excess - Rabbi Meir vs Rabbi Natan Dispute

micro_segments.title: Case: Collected More Than Needed for Poor Person's Burial, Person Actually Had Funds
Rav Yirmiyah: Excess Goes to Heirs; Rav Idi Disagrees - Whole Collection Was Mistake
Baraita: Rabbi Natan Rule - Use Excess to Build Tombstone and Perfume Coffin
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel: Don't Build Tombstones for Righteous, Their Teaching Is Memorial

macro_segments.title: Narrative: Rabbi Yochanan's Encounter with Rabbi Eliezer from Babylon

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan Sees Rabbi Eliezer Approaching, Eliezer Hides Out of Respect
Rabbi Yochanan Complains: Eliezer Didn't Greet Him and Hid from Him
Rabbi Yaakov bar Idi Explains: Babylonian Custom - Younger Doesn't Greet Greater
Scripture Support: 'Young Saw Me and Hid, Elders Rose and Stood'
Analogy: Like Idol - Show No Honor by Avoiding It, Close Eyes and Pass
Conclusion: Rabbi Eliezer's Hiding Showed Respect, Not Disrespect
